{{Quote|The echoes of the Fanged God can be heard throughout the air of Dathomir, from the sound of the snuffling moogs hidden in their lairs to the screeches of the archixes circling their aeries atop the Singing Mountain.|An extract from ''Wild Power'' by Mother Talzin|<ref name="BOS" />}}
'''Moogs''' were a small [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of creatures [[Homeworld/Legends|indigenous]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] that were the favored food of the [[Reptomammal/Legends|reptomammalian]] [[Rancor/Legends|rancors]]. They were known to be quite difficult to catch as they were both swift and fast-moving animals.<ref name="TCSWE" /> They made a snuffling sound and lived in lairs. [[Mother (title)/Legends|Mother]] [[Talzin/Legends|Talzin]] of the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsister]] [[Coven/Legends|coven]] of [[Witches of Dathomir|Dathomir Witches]] mentioned the creatures in her manifesto, ''[[Wild Power]]'', comparing their snuffles to the echoes of the [[Son/Legends|Fanged God]],<ref name="BOS" /> a [[Celestial/Legends|being]] of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] who resided in the realm of [[Mortis/Legends|Mortis]]<ref name="Son Encyclopedia">{{SWE|characters|son|Son}}</ref> and was worshipped by the Nightsisters as a [[Deity/Legends|deity]].<ref name="BOS" />

''Wild Power'' was one of many writings collected in the ''[[Book of Sith]]'', a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] historical book assembled by [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] sometime between [[19 BBY]] and [[18 BBY]].<ref name="BOS" /> The term to "[[List of phrases and slang/Legends|play moog-and-rancor]]" referred to a situation in which one party was chasing another, specifically during [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfights]].<ref name="Tempest">''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Moogs were first mentioned in ''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'', the first novel in ''[[Star Wars: The Dark Nest Trilogy]]'', written by [[Troy Denning]] and published in [[2005]].<ref name="DNI">''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]''</ref>
